title: Sonnet 40 - Oh, yes! they love through all this world of ours! by Elizabeth Barrett Browning — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
Oh, yes! they love through all this world of ours!
I will not gainsay love, called love forsooth.
I have heard love talked in my early youth,
And since, not so long back but that the flowers
Then gathered, smell still. Mussulmans and Giaours
Throw kerchiefs at a smile, and have no ruth
For any weeping. Polypheme's white tooth
Slips on the nut if, after frequent showers,
meaning: "Sonnet 40 - Oh, yes! they love through all this world of ours!" by Elizabeth Barrett Browning is a sad and love poem consisting of 14 lines. The poet explores themes of sad and love through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "Oh, yes! they love through all this world of ours! I will not gainsay love, called love forsooth....", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Elizabeth Barrett Browning's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
